tree.title: 1.5 Related Documents

tree.title: 2. CASA - An Overview
tree.summary: This section provides an overview of the Current Account and Savings Account (CASA) sub-system in the Oracle FLEXCUBE system, detailing its functions such as managing cheque books, tracking stop payment instructions, and defining amount blocks.

tree.title: 2.1 Major Features
tree.summary: This section provides an overview of the Current Account and Savings Account (CASA) sub-system in the Oracle FLEXCUBE system, detailing its functions such as managing cheque books, tracking stop payment instructions, and defining amount blocks.

tree.title: 2.2 Major Functions
tree.summary: This page details the major functions of a system for managing cheque books and transactions, including maintenance, processing alerts, reports generation, and purging old records.

tree.title: 3. Maintaining Account Class
tree.summary: This section describes how to maintain account classes and includes details on maintaining account classes, invoking the maintenance screen, and using different tabs within the screen.

tree.title: 3.1 Account Class Maintenance
tree.summary: This section describes how to maintain account classes and includes details on maintaining account classes, invoking the maintenance screen, and using different tabs within the screen.

tree.title: 3.1.1 Maintaining Account Classes
tree.summary: This section describes how to maintain account classes and includes details on maintaining account classes, invoking the maintenance screen, and using different tabs within the screen.

tree.title: 3.1.2 Invoking Account Class Maintenance Screen
tree.summary: This page describes how account classes are defined and maintained in a banking system, including attributes like general ledger lines, statement generation frequency, and interest rate structures, and how these classes are linked to customer accounts.

tree.title: 3.1.3 Statement Parameters Tab
tree.summary: This page discusses the Real Time Liquidity feature and its restrictions for account classes, as well as the Statement Parameters tab for defining statement generation preferences.

tree.title: 3.1.4 Preferences Tab
tree.summary: This page outlines the details that need to be captured for a Specially Designated National (SDN), including their name, date of birth, country of origin, remarks, and addresses, as well as introduces the Preferences Tab for specifying account class preferences.

tree.title: 3.1.5 GL Lines Button
tree.summary: This page discusses system configurations for handling unprinted transactions and account statuses in Oracle FLEXCUBE, including settings for collateral linkage, NPA identification, and general ledger line assignments.

tree.title: 3.1.6 Auto Deposits Button
tree.summary: This section explains how to set up auto deposit accounts for savings accounts and describes the fields that need to be specified on the 'Auto Deposit' screen.

tree.title: 3.1.7 Status Rule Button
tree.summary: This page describes how to specify deposit account class details and handle batches for automatic deposit creation, as well as setting up status rules for accounts.

tree.title: 3.1.8 Amount Block Button
tree.summary: This page explains how Oracle FLEXCUBE updates account statuses and flags related to SOD (Short-term Overdue) debit interest payments, and also describes the processing of OD status change based on account interim days.

tree.title: 3.1.9 Restriction Button
tree.summary: This section explains how to place restrictions on transactions involving customer accounts and describes the allocation of funds within contracts based on various criteria.

tree.title: 3.1.10 Statement Button
tree.summary: This page describes how to set up product and transaction code restrictions for account classes, validate these restrictions during operations, and generate account balance details using the statement screen.

tree.title: 3.1.11 REG Button
tree.summary: This section explains how to define Regulation D limits for an account class and specifies the details that can be set through the 'REG' button on the 'Account Class Maintenance' screen.

tree.title: 3.1.12 Branches/Currencies Button
tree.summary: This section explains how to set branch and currency preferences for account classes, including specifying allowed or disallowed branches and currencies, and maintaining these preferences through the 'Account Class – Branch and Currency Preferences' screen.

tree.title: 3.1.13 Customer Categories Button
tree.summary: This page describes how to set up and validate minimum balance and opening balance requirements for different account facilities in a direct banking context.

tree.title: 3.1.14 Notice Button
tree.summary: This page describes how to set notice preferences for account classes, including options for advance interest, monthly free amount, and notice days.

tree.title: 3.1.15 Banking Channels Button
tree.summary: This page discusses specifying the validity period for bank notices and how to maintain details of various banking channels at the customer level.

tree.title: 3.1.16 Default Limits
tree.summary: This page describes the system checks and default settings for account channels and limits in a customer/account management process.

tree.title: 3.1.17 Dormancy Button
tree.summary: This page discusses interest parameters for a CASA account and the process of moving balances to dormancy status at EOD.

tree.title: 3.2 Account Class Entitlement/Restriction Maintenance
tree.summary: This section describes how to maintain account class entitlements and restrictions for various preferences including branch details, currency details, categories, customers, and accounts.

tree.summary: This page explains how to manage branch and currency preferences for account classes by allowing or disallowing specific branches and currencies.

tree.summary: This page explains how to manage branch and currency preferences for account classes by allowing or disallowing specific branches and currencies.

tree.title: 3.2.3 Customer Categories Button
tree.summary: This page explains how to specify minimum balance and opening balance for facility combinations in a currency, considering account level and class level settings, and default values if no matching records are found.

tree.title: 3.2.4 Account Entitlement
tree.summary: This page explains how to configure an account class by specifying customer categories and individual customers who can or cannot use the features of that class, using NostroLCY as an example.

tree.title: 3.3 Account Class Denomination Deposit
tree.summary: This page details how to specify payment and branch entitlement groups and how to invoke the 'Account Class Denomination Deposit' screen.

tree.title: 3.4 Account Class Group Maintenance
tree.summary: This section explains how to maintain and view account class groups in the 'Account Class Group Maintenance' screen.

tree.summary: This section explains how to maintain and view account class groups in the 'Account Class Group Maintenance' screen.

tree.title: 3.4.2 Viewing Account Class Group
tree.summary: This page describes how to specify and manage account class groups in a system, including selecting account classes and viewing details through a specific screen.

tree.title: 3.5 Account Class Provisioning Maintenance
tree.summary: This section explains how to maintain account class provisioning by creating rules based on various elements such as outstanding balance, receivable interest, and account status.

tree.title: 3.5.1 Maintaining Account Class Provisioning
tree.summary: This section explains how to maintain account class provisioning by creating rules based on various elements such as outstanding balance, receivable interest, and account status.

tree.title: 3.6 Account Class Transfer

tree.summary: This page describes the process of maintaining and transferring account classes in Oracle FLEXCUBE, including how to initiate the transfer and details maintained on the main tab.

tree.summary: This page describes the process of maintaining and transferring account classes in Oracle FLEXCUBE, including how to initiate the transfer and details maintained on the main tab.

tree.title: 3.6.4 Processing Account Class Transfer
tree.summary: This page describes how to maintain credit and debit details for GL, HO, and CB lines, as well as the process of changing account class at the customer level without altering the account number.

tree.title: 3.6.5 Viewing Account Class Transfer
tree.summary: This page describes the process of processing batch operations for account class transfer during the BOD process and how to view the summary of such transfers.

tree.title: 3.7 Account Class Value Date Maintenance
tree.summary: This page describes how to perform a query using various parameters and details displayed for matching records, as well as explaining the value date maintenance process for different account types and transactions.

tree.title: 3.7.1 Maintaining Account Class Value Dates
tree.summary: This section explains how to maintain account class value dates by defining parameters such as account class, description, type, holiday treatment, transaction indicator, offset days, and start day of the slab.

tree.summary: This page explains date-related settings for financial transactions and account statuses, including offset treatments and value dates, as well as how to manually change account statuses.

tree.title: 3.8.1 Modifying Account Status Manually
tree.summary: This page explains date-related settings for financial transactions and account statuses, including offset treatments and value dates, as well as how to manually change account statuses.

tree.title: 3.9 Maintaining Turnover Limits
tree.summary: This page describes various options and settings for managing account statuses, including automatic status changes, transaction overrides, and bancontrol statuses.

tree.title: 4. Customer Accounts
tree.summary: This page describes how to maintain customer accounts in Oracle FLEXCUBE, including account number structure, parameters definition, and authorization process.

tree.title: 4.1 Customer Account Maintenance
tree.summary: This page discusses various sections related to customer account maintenance and track receivable management, including opening accounts, maintaining different tabs within the account, releasing and deleting track receivables, and generating reports.

tree.title: 4.1.1 Opening Customer Accounts
tree.summary: This page provides an overview of invoking the 'Customer Accounts Maintenance' screen and its default display, including how to open it and the initial view shown.

tree.title: 4.1.2 Main Tab
tree.summary: This page details the account setup process for Multi Currency Account (MCA) customers, including fields and options available on the customer screen.

tree.title: 4.1.3 Auxiliary Tab
tree.summary: This page describes how to configure settings for cheque book management, including automatic reordering and specifying preferences such as reorder level and number of leaves.

tree.title: 4.1.4 Nominee Tab
tree.summary: This page describes how to specify authorization status and expiry date for a debit interest liquidation preference, as well as details required for capturing nominee information.

tree.title: 4.1.5 Checklist Tab
tree.summary: This page outlines the details required for specifying a guardian for a minor nominee and provides instructions on using the Checklist tab to maintain document types.

tree.title: 4.1.6 Capturing Additional Details
tree.summary: This section details various buttons available in the 'Customer Accounts Maintenance' screen that allow users to access different maintenance screens for additional account information.

tree.title: 4.1.10 BIC Button
tree.summary: This page describes various settings and options for configuring charge consolidations, including currency, minimum and maximum charges, enabling or disabling setups, waiving charges, applying discounts as flat amounts or percentages, and managing SWIFT BICs for customer accounts.

tree.title: 4.1.11 Instructions Button

tree.title: 4.1.14 REG Button
tree.summary: This section explains how to use the 'Linked Entities' button in the 'Customer Accounts Maintenance' screen to establish relationships between customers and accounts, including selecting a customer, setting a relationship code, and configuring signature verification.

tree.title: 4.1.15 Account Status Button
tree.summary: This section explains how to use the 'Status Details' screen by clicking the 'Account Status' button in the 'Customer Accounts Maintenance' screen to capture various account status details.

tree.title: 4.1.16 Restriction Button
tree.summary: This page describes how to specify reporting lines and restrictions for accounts, including central bank lines, head office GLs, and product transaction code restrictions.

tree.title: 4.1.17 Currency Limits Button
tree.summary: This page describes how to set up product and currency restrictions for customer accounts through specific screens in a system.

tree.title: 4.1.18 MIS Button

tree.title: 4.1.19 Statement Button

tree.title: 4.1.20 Limits Button

This page introduces the User Manual for Oracle FLEXCUBE's Current and Savings Account sub-module, detailing its purpose, target audience, accessibility information, and chapter organization.
pages.raw_text:
1-1
1. Preface
1.1 
Introduction
This manual is designed to help you quickly get acquainted with the Current and Savings 
Account sub-module of Oracle FLEXCUBE.
It provides an overview to the module, and provides information on using the Current and 
Savings Account sub-module of Oracle FLEXCUBE. 
Besides this User Manual, you can obtain information specific to a particular field by placing 
the cursor on the relevant field and striking <F1> on the keyboard.
1.2 
Audience
This manual is intended for the following User/User Roles:
1.3 
Documentation Accessibility
For information about Oracle's commitment to accessibility, visit the Oracle Accessibility 
Program website at http://www.oracle.com/pls/topic/lookup?ctx=acc&id=docacc.
1.4 
Organization
This manual is organized into the following chapters:
Role
Function
Back office clerk
Input functions for contracts
Back office managers/officers
Authorization functions
Product Managers
Product definition and authorization
End of day operators
Processing during end of day/ beginning of day
Financial Controller / Product Managers
Generation of reports
Chapter
Description
Chapter 1
Preface gives information on the intended audience. It also lists 
the various chapters covered in this User Manual.
Chapter 2
CASA - an Overview is a snapshot of the features that the module 
provides.
Chapter 3 
Maintaining Account Class talks on how to maintain account 
classes for a customer account.
Chapter 4
Maintaining Customer Accounts explains how you can maintain 
accounts in your bank for customers
Chapter 5
Maintaining Tax Allowance Maintenance explains how you can 
maintain tax-free allowance limit at the customer account level

1-2
Chapter 6
Maintaining Stamp Duty Taxation explains the workflow of Stamp 
Duty Taxation, and the options to define the same.
Chapter 7
Maintaining Passbook  describes the information required for issu-
ance of passbook to customers.
Chapter 8
Maintaining Cheque Book Details gives information pertaining to 
the maintenance of Cheque books issued to customers. 
Chapter 9
Maintaining Account Structure explains the account structure sup-
ported in Oracle FLEXCUBE.
Chapter 10
Account Switching Service explains about account switch services 
supported by Oracle FLEXCUBE.
Chapter 11
Maintaining Amount Blocks gives details on maintaining amount 
blocks placed on customer accounts. 
Chapter 12
Maintaining Stop Payment Details explains how you maintain 
details of stop payment instructions on Cheques issued by cus-
tomers. 
Chapter 13
Generating Account Statements explains the maintenances for 
account statement generation.
Chapter 14
Processing Post Dated Cheques describes how Oracle FLEX-
CUBE processes post dated Cheques. 
Chapter 15
Instrument Details explains how the reference information needed 
for entry of instrument-based transactions in the Oracle FLEX-
CUBE Branch system can be maintained.
Chapter 16
Delinquency Maintenance explains about the maintenances of 
delinquency product details.
Chapter 17
Annual Percentage Yield for CASA accounts explains how Annual 
Percentage Yield (APY) on customer accounts is processed in 
Oracle FLEXCUBE
Chapter 18
Batch Processing describes the various batch operations done in 
this module which are required for batch processing.
Chapter 19
Annexure A details format of ‘PD_PDC_CAN’ message.
Chapter 20
Annexure B - Accounting Entries gives the event-wise accounting 
entries for CASA module.
Chapter 21
Annexure C – IC Rule Set-up gives the IC rule set-up for CASA 
module.
Chapter 22
Annexure D - Tags  lists the tags supported by different modules 
to update the remarks during account statement generation.
Chapter 23
Annexure E - Functions Supporting Financial Inclusion lists the 
category of  financial roles who can acces specific Function ID of 
Oracle FLEXCUBE.
Chapter 24
Glossary gives a list of important terms used in the manual.

pages.summary: This section provides an overview of the Current Account and Savings Account (CASA) sub-system in the Oracle FLEXCUBE system, detailing its functions such as managing cheque books, tracking stop payment instructions, and defining amount blocks.
pages.raw_text:
2-1
2. CASA - An Overview
Current Account and Savings Account, commonly referred to as CASA, is one of the modular 
sub-systems in the Oracle FLEXCUBE system. Through this system your bank can execute 
the following functions:
Create a customer account
Capture details of a Cheque book issued to a customer
For each Cheque book, the details of which you have maintained, the system generates 
a status matrix. This matrix stores the status of each leaf of the Cheque book. When no 
transaction has taken place the status of the Cheque is stored as ‘unused’. Whenever 
a Cheque based transaction is initiated by Data Entry modules the status of this Cheque 
moves from ‘unused’ to ‘used’ in this matrix. Subsequently, if a Cheque is canceled or 
rejected the status can be updated in the status matrix.
Apart from this, tracking and upholding a stop payment instruction against Cheques, is 
also a function that this sub-system supports. These stop payment instructions, 
normally coming from a customer against a Cheque or a series of Cheque or an amount 
for which a Cheque was issued; are applicable for a period or indefinitely. 
Yet another feature supported by CASA is the facility to define amount blocks for accounts 
(as per a customer’s instruction or the instruction of the bank). The system processes the 
expiry of such blocks automatically.
To put it briefly in the CASA sub-system you maintain the following details:
Cheque books issued to customers
Amount blocks enforced against accounts
Stop payment instructions issued by customers
These details are maintained in what are called data tables and are called forth for interface 
during a Cheque processing by the Data Entry system. These tables are:
‘Cheque Book Details’, ‘Cheque Status’ and ‘Cheque Details’ where you maintain 
Cheque book details
‘Amount block maintenance’ where you maintain amount blocks against particular 
amounts
‘Stop Payment’ where you maintain stop payment instructions issued against a Cheque 
or range of Cheques
This chapter contains the following sections:
Section 2.1, "Major Features"
Section 2.2, "Major Functions"
2.1 
Major Features
There are three major features this system provides:
A service which returns the status of the Cheque
A service which updates the status of the Cheque
A service which will process initiation and expiry of stop payment instructions and 
amount blocks

pages.summary: This page details the major functions of a system for managing cheque books and transactions, including maintenance, processing alerts, reports generation, and purging old records.
pages.raw_text:
2-2
2.2 
Major Functions
The major functions, which the system provides, are the following:
Function
Details
Maintenance
It provides for maintenance of Cheque books issued for a customer 
account, with a provision to track and update the status of each 
Cheque leaf in the book
Maintenance of stop payment instructions issued by customers based 
on, either, Cheque numbers, or, the amount for which the Cheque, or 
Cheques, were issued
A facility to maintain amount blocks on customer accounts
Processing
Whenever a Cheque-based transaction is initiated, the system will alert 
the user about the status of the Cheque
In case of a stop payment instruction enforced against that account 
number, the system will verify whether the details of the Cheque and 
the stop payment instructions match. It will warn the user accordingly, 
and ask for an override
In case of amount blocks, the system takes care of the minimum bal-
ance to be maintained in the account while monitoring Limits.
The system allows the debit transactions initiated from branch over the 
counter or through inward clearing to utilize the uncollected funds when 
the transaction amount exceeds the account balance by doing a force 
debit against the uncollected funds.
Reports
Two types of reports can be generated:
Details of all Cheques processed along with their status.
Details of stop payment instructions enforced against various accounts.
Details of records that have been purged.
Purge
The user has the facility to purge old records which are no longer being 
used.

pages.summary: This section describes how to maintain account classes and includes details on maintaining account classes, invoking the maintenance screen, and using different tabs within the screen.
pages.raw_text:
3-1
3. Maintaining Account Class
While defining CIF records and Customer Accounts, you would have noticed that many of the 
fields have option lists positioned next to them. While some option lists contain pre-defined 
values for some option lists to be populated you need to capture data through separate 
screens. For instance, while maintaining CIF records you need to associate each customer 
with a customer category. For this purpose you will need to maintain customer categories 
before you begin to maintain CIF records. Similarly, each customer account needs to be 
associated with an account class. Therefore, you will need to maintain account classes before 
you start defining customer accounts.
This chapter contains the following sections:
Section 3.1, "Account Class Maintenance"
Section 3.2, "Account Class Entitlement/Restriction Maintenance"
Section 3.3, "Account Class Denomination Deposit"
Section 3.4, "Account Class Group Maintenance"
Section 3.5, "Account Class Provisioning Maintenance"
Section 3.6, "Account Class Transfer"
Section 3.7, "Account Class Value Date Maintenance"
Section 3.8, "Manual Status Change Input"
Section 3.9, "Maintaining Turnover Limits"
3.1 
Account Class Maintenance
This section contains the following topics:
Section 3.1.1, "Maintaining Account Classes"
Section 3.1.2, "Invoking Account Class Maintenance Screen"
